name: packmol
description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "create a packmol input", "pack molecules with packmol", "solvate a protein", "build an initial configuration", "setup molecular dynamics", or discusses molecular packing, solvation, or building simulation starting structures.
capabilities: Generate Packmol input files programmatically from Python
Validate input syntax before running Packmol
Solvate proteins with water and ions automatically
Check output PDB files for atomic overlaps
author: Chenghao-Wu
securitySummary: Legitimate scientific tool for molecular dynamics simulation preparation. Static analyzer flagged documentation examples and coordinate syntax as security issues. All findings are false positives: README command examples were misidentified as shell execution, molecular coordinates were flagged as path traversal, and the random seed parameter was misidentified as cryptography. No malicious intent, credential access, or data exfiltration detected.
valueStatement: Building initial molecular structures for simulations requires precise spatial constraints. Packmol automates molecule placement with overlap prevention, supporting boxes, spheres, cylinders, and planes for diverse simulation needs.
limitations: Packmol software must be installed separately via pip
Requires existing molecule structure files (PDB, XYZ, or TINKER format)
Does not perform actual molecular dynamics simulation
Limited to predefined constraint types supported by Packmol

useCases.title: Protein solvation setup
useCases.description: Solvate biomolecules with water and ions for MD simulation preparation

useCases.title: Interface system building
useCases.description: Create liquid-liquid or liquid-vapor interfaces using plane constraints

useCases.title: Molecular packing basics
useCases.description: Learn to pack simple molecular boxes with multiple molecule types

promptTemplates.title: Basic box packing
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Packmol input file to pack 1000 water molecules in a 40x40x40 Angstrom box with 2.0 Angstrom tolerance

promptTemplates.title: Protein solvation
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a Packmol input file to solvate protein.pdb with 5000 water molecules, 10 Na+ ions, and 10 Cl- ions using 15 Angstrom solvent shell

promptTemplates.title: Interface system
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an input file for a water/chloroform interface with PBC at z=-30 to 30, placing 1000 water below plane and 200 chloroform above plane

promptTemplates.title: Automated solvation
promptTemplates.prompt: Use solvate_helper.py to generate a complete Packmol solvation input for my protein with 15 Angstrom shell, +4 charge, and 0.15 M salt concentration

bestPractices: Start with small test systems (10-50 molecules) before scaling up
Validate input files with validate_input.py before running Packmol
Set a random seed (e.g., seed 12345) for reproducible results
Check output for overlaps using check_overlaps.py after packing
antiPatterns: Running Packmol without validating input syntax first
Using a tolerance value smaller than 1.5 Angstrom for all-atom models
Skipping overlap checks on output structures before simulations
Creating very large systems without using restart files

faq.question: Is Packmol installed with this skill?
faq.answer: No. Install separately with: pip install packmol

faq.question: What molecule file formats are supported?
How many molecules can Packmol handle?
faq.answer: Practical limit is around 100,000 atoms. Use restart files for larger systems.

faq.question: Does this skill run Packmol or just generate inputs?
faq.answer: The skill generates input files. Run Packmol separately with: packmol < input.inp

faq.question: Can I check if my output has problems?
faq.answer: Use scripts like check_overlaps.py to verify atomic distances and analyze_density.py for density.

faq.question: How is this different from other MD setup tools?
faq.answer: Packmol focuses on initial structure packing. Use with GROMACS or AMBER for force field setup and energy minimization.
